incident.title: SMB Relay — NTLM Relay Attempt and Hash Capture Fallback
incident.status: Documented — Lab Exercise (SMB signing blocked shell — hash capture fallback executed)
incident.outcome: SMB signing on DC01 blocked relay shell. NTLMv2 hash captured as fallback. jsmith:Password123! recovered.
mitre_techniques: T1557.001 - LLMNR/NBT-NS Poisoning and SMB Relay

kill_chain.name: LLMNR Poisoning — Responder SMB Off
kill_chain.config_change: SMB = Off and HTTP = Off in /etc/responder/Responder.conf
kill_chain.result: Win11 LLMNR broadcast poisoned. Auth redirected to Kali 10.10.10.99. ntlmrelayx receives incoming connection.
kill_chain.detection_note: VIS-001 — intra-LAN traffic never routes through OPNsense/Suricata.

kill_chain.name: NTLM Relay Attempt to DC01
kill_chain.result: Relay reached DC01. Rejected — SMB signing required. Session not established for command execution.
kill_chain.relay_outcome: BLOCKED — Message signing enabled and required on DC01
kill_chain.detection_note: EC4624, NTLM, Logon_Type=3, src_ip=10.10.10.99. NTLM Type 3 logon from Linux IP for domain account.

kill_chain.name: Hash Capture Fallback
kill_chain.result: NTLMv2 hash for jsmith captured during relay exchange before signing rejection.
kill_chain.detection_note: Hash captured at network layer — no endpoint log generated.

kill_chain.name: Offline Hash Cracking
kill_chain.detection_note: Offline operation — no logging system can detect this.

smb_signing_check.implication: Relay-to-shell blocked on DC01. Pivot required to member servers or workstations for full relay.

splunk_evidence.detection: NTLM relay — domain account from attacker IP
splunk_evidence.query: index=windows EventCode=4624 Authentication_Package=NTLM Logon_Type=3 | where NOT match(src_ip, "^10\.10\.10\.(40|30|1)$") | table _time, Account_Name, src_ip

detections.name: NTLM relay — domain account from attacker IP
detections.filter: Authentication_Package=NTLM AND Logon_Type=3 AND src_ip not in domain infrastructure

detections.name: NTLM spike from single non-domain IP
detections.filter: Count of NTLM Type 3 from same src_ip above threshold

detections.name: SMB signing rejection at network layer

visibility_gaps.description: Suricata blind to intra-LAN relay traffic
visibility_gaps.impact: SMB relay negotiation invisible at network layer

visibility_gaps.description: If relay target is workstation, events only on that host
visibility_gaps.impact: Detection moves to target workstation — needs UF there
visibility_gaps.fix: Ensure Splunk UF on all Windows hosts

key_lesson: DC01 SMB signing enforced by default — correct posture. Real attack pivots to member servers or workstations where signing is enabled but not required. Enforcing SMB signing via GPO on ALL hosts eliminates relay entirely.
recommendations: Enforce SMB signing on ALL hosts via GPO — not just DCs
Disable LLMNR and NBT-NS domain-wide via GPO
Deploy Splunk UF on win11-vm to capture victim-side events
Monitor for NTLM Type 3 logons from non-domain-joined IP addresses
Consider deploying 802.1X network authentication to prevent rogue devices
